meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Finish Your First Student App
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master advanced development workflows and build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves unfinished projects by enforcing modular, atomic architecture. By combining this rigorous framework with Claude Code’s autonomous execution, you bridge the gap between ideation and deployment. This system forces completion by breaking complex features into executable, verifiable micro-tasks, ensuring you ship functional code every single session.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a folder full of abandoned repositories, half-baked READMEs, and the mounting weight of 'what if I just started over?' The graveyard of student projects is paved with good intentions and broken architecture. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a system that handles the friction of growth.

Today, we stop the cycle. We are deploying the Antigravity project template—a structural blueprint designed to maintain momentum even when complexity spikes. By pairing this with the raw power of Claude Code, you will transform your development process from a chaotic struggle into a predictable, high-velocity engineering machine.

steps.title: Initialize Modular Architecture
steps.description: Establish the project skeleton using the Antigravity structure to ensure separation of concerns from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity standard, focusing on a clean repository structure with isolated modules for API, database, and UI logic.

steps.title: Define Atomic Feature Specs
steps.description: Decompose your feature list into individual JSON-defined tasks that Antigravity can track.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a manifest.json file containing 5 atomic tasks for a MVP, ensuring each task has a clear success condition and dependency chain.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate manifest.json with 5 atomic project tasks'

steps.title: Execute Core Logic via Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to implement the first core module without getting bogged down in boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the core authentication service using the Antigravity module pattern. Ensure all error handling follows the project's standard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/auth.ts --prompt 'Implement user login logic'

steps.title: Automated Integration Testing
steps.description: Run automated verification to ensure your new code doesn't break the existing scaffold.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests for the current module, targeting edge cases in the user authentication flow.

steps.title: Final Build and Deployment
steps.description: Compile the project into a production-ready artifact using the Antigravity build pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the production build pipeline to optimize assets and generate a deployment-ready Dockerfile.

pro_tips: Never write code without an associated test case; the Antigravity template thrives on TDD.
Use Claude Code's 'review' flag on every PR to catch architecture drift before it becomes technical debt.
Keep your context window clean by clearing your chat history once a major milestone is reached.
Treat your manifest.json as the single source of truth; if it isn't in the manifest, it doesn't exist.
Focus on 'shipping' over 'perfecting'—deploy a broken feature behind a feature flag rather than letting it rot in a branch.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is live, use the Antigravity template to add a secondary service, effectively turning your project into a distributed system.
content.conversion_block: Don't let another project sit in your repository graveyard. Master the tools that bridge the gap between learning and shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
